sourceMovie.title: Dirty Rotten Scoundrels
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Dirty Rotten Scoundrels works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Guys and Dolls
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dirty Rotten Scoundrels clicked for you, Guys and Dolls (1955) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Catch Me If You Can
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dirty Rotten Scoundrels clicked for you, Catch Me If You Can (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: RocknRolla
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dirty Rotten Scoundrels clicked for you, RocknRolla (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Dirty Rotten Scoundrels?
faq.answer: Try Guys and Dolls, Catch Me If You Can, RocknRolla—each shares DNA with Dirty Rotten Scoundrels while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Dirty Rotten Scoundrels?
faq.answer: Dirty Rotten Scoundrels (1988) is best described as Comedy, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Dirty Rotten Scoundrels worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
